page: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance powered by the x86_64 AMD EPYC-Rome processor architecture. The configuration features 16 physical cores mapped to 16 vCPUs with a single thread per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. This instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with the instance, providing 2560 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. No dedicated graphics processing units are included in this hardware profile.

In synthetic benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates distinct performance characteristics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated as strong, placing in the top 25% of tested instances with a score of 2927.78. Conversely, the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance registers an average rating in the middle 50% with a score of 46301.39. This performance profile indicates robust single-threaded execution capabilities, though multi-threaded scaling is moderate, likely influenced by the shared CPU allocation model.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers high memory and storage capacity relative to its compute footprint, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring substantial RAM and fast local storage without requiring dedicated CPU threads. The combination of 128.0 GB of RAM and 2560 GB of NVMe SSD makes this instance suitable for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, caching layers, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a generous 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 2560 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model. In benchmark testing, it delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% with a stress-ng score of 2927.78, while its multi-core performance is average. Lacking a GPU, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, and caching systems that benefit from high memory density and fast local storage.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2560 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring high-capacity NVMe storage and strong single-core performance for data-intensive applications.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
2560 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
